mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in quarterly GDP (GDPC1) signals strong economic demand, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 60% of periods when GDPC1 growth exceeds 3% YoY.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent monthly CPI inflation above 2.5% triggers central bank rate hikes, increasing discount rates and compressing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX records positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL indicate a tightening cycle, raising borrowing costs and reducing corporate margins, which predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 20d returns in > 40% of periods with concurrent FEDFUNDS > 2% and CPIAUCSL > 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in UNRATE below 4% signals tight labor markets, increasing wage pressures and inflation expectations, which predicts negative S&P 500 returns during monetary tightening regimes.
failure_condition: SPX records positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where UNRATE falls below 4% during Fed rate hikes.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in PAYEMS above 150k signals robust job creation, boosting consumer spending and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows negative 5d returns in > 50% of periods with PAYEMS growth exceeding 150k.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in INDPRO above 105 signals strong industrial output, which predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days by signaling economic resilience.
failure_condition: SPX records negative 20d returns in > 50% of instances where INDPRO exceeds 105.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in HOUST and RSAFS indicates strong demand for housing and consumer goods, which predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days via improved corporate revenue outlook.
failure_condition: SPX shows negative 5d returns in > 40% of periods with simultaneous HOUST > 1200k and RSAFS > 490k.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in FEDFUNDS below 1.5% signals monetary easing, reducing risk-free rates and boosting equity valuations, which predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX records negative 60d returns in > 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS falls below 1.5%.
